commands.review_summary:
The branch carries `SessionEntry.estimatedCostUsd` into status summary data and adds a formatted Cost column to the CLI status sessions table.

PR surface: Source +4. Total +4 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers. Source inspection and live CI logs show the new Cost field breaks typed status fixtures, exact row expectations, and a rendered status-output expectation; I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof, and maintainers should decide whether this PR should remain a CLI-only partial or be expanded to the linked slash `/status` request.

target.title: feat(status): display session cost in /status output (fixes #64334)
target.body:
feat(status): display session cost in /status output (fixes #64334)

## What

Add a `Cost` column to the session table in `openclaw status` output, showing the cumulative estimated cost per session in USD.

## Why

Users currently have no way to see per-session API cost without manually parsing transcript JSONL files. The data (`estimatedCostUsd`) already exists in the session entry â€” this PR surfaces it in the status display.

## How

- Add `estimatedCostUsd: number | null` to `SessionStatus` type
- Populate from `entry?.estimatedCostUsd` in `getStatusSummary`
- Add `Cost` column to the sessions table with formatted USD display
- Show `$X.XX` when cost data is available, muted `â€”` otherwise

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** /status output now includes a Cost column showing per-session estimated API cost
- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4, OpenClaw local build from upstream/main
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Verified all 4 modified files contain the expected `estimatedCostUsd` references
  2. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/status.summary.test.ts` â€” 14 tests passed
  3. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/status-runtime-shared.test.ts` â€” 14 tests passed
  4. Ran `pnpm build` â€” built successfully in 80s
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n "estimatedCostUsd" src/commands/status.types.ts src/commands/status.summary.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.ts src/commands/status.command-report-data.ts
src/commands/status.types.ts:41:  estimatedCostUsd: number | null;
src/commands/status.summary.ts:421:          estimatedCostUsd: entry?.estimatedCostUsd ?? null,
src/commands/status.command-sections.ts:358:    Cost: sess.estimatedCostUsd != null ? `$${sess.estimatedCostUsd.toFixed(2)}` : params.muted("â€”"),

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/status.summary.test.ts
 âś“  commands  src/commands/status.summary.test.ts (14 tests) 1267ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)

$ pnpm build
âś“ built in 491ms
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Session table in `/status` output includes a Cost column. Tests pass, build succeeds.
- **What was not tested:** Live runtime cost accumulation (requires running gateway with paid provider). The `estimatedCostUsd` field is already populated by the session store â€” this PR only surfaces it in the UI.
